logical_units.text:
भारतीय įरज़वर् बैंक RESERVE BANK OF INDIA
RBI/DoR/2025-26/322 DoR.LRG.REC.No.241/13-10-005/2025-26


 November 28, 2025
Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Resource Raising
Norms) Directions, 2025

logical_units.title: Chapter III - Repeal and Other Provisions ......................................................................... 7
logical_units.text:
Chapter III - Repeal and Other Provisions ......................................................................... 7
A.
Repeal and Saving .................................................................................................. 7
B.
Application of other laws not barred .................................................................... 7
C.
Interpretations ........................................................................................................ 7
<image>
In exercise of the powers conferred under Section 45L of the Reserve Bank of India
Act, 1934, and all other provisions / laws enabling the Reserve Bank of India ('RBI') in
this regard, RBI being satisfied that it is necessary and expedient in the public interest
so to do, hereby, issues the Directions hereinafter specified.

logical_units.title: 1. These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
logical_units.text:
1. These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
Institutions - Resource Raising Norms) Directions, 2025

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 2. These Directions shall become effective on the day these are placed on the official
logical_units.text:
2. These Directions shall become effective on the day these are placed on the official
website of the Reserve Bank.

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word

logical_units.title: B. Applicability
logical_units.text: B. Applicability

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 3. These Directions shall be applicable to the All India Financial Institutions (AIFIs)
logical_units.text:
3. These Directions shall be applicable to the All India Financial Institutions (AIFIs)
regulated by the Reserve Bank, viz., the Export-Import Bank of India (EXIM Bank),
the National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD), the National
Bank for Financing Infrastructure and Development (NaBFID), the National
Housing Bank (NHB), and the Small Industries Development Bank of India (SIDBI).
<image>

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: Chapter II - Resource Raising Norms
logical_units.text: Chapter II - Resource Raising Norms

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 4. The RBI has prescribed an 'Umbrella Limit' (UL), for the mobilisation of resources
logical_units.text:
4. The RBI has prescribed an 'Umbrella Limit' (UL), for the mobilisation of resources
by an AIFI through specified instruments, linked to the 'Net Owned Funds' (NOF)
of the AIFI concerned. This UL formed part of the 'Overall Borrowing Limit' (OBL)
and was also based on NOF. With the introduction of the Leverage Ratio under
Basel III Capital Regulations w.e.f., April 1, 2024, the OBL has been dispensed
with. However, an AIFI shall continue to adhere to the UL.

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: A. Umbrella Limit (UL)
logical_units.text: A. Umbrella Limit (UL)

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 5. The aggregate borrowings under the UL shall not at any time exceed 100 per cent
logical_units.text:
5. The aggregate borrowings under the UL shall not at any time exceed 100 per cent
of the NOF of an AIFI as per its latest audited balance sheet, or as approved by
the RBI.

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 6. The UL shall consist of borrowings through four specified instruments, viz., term
logical_units.text:
6. The UL shall consist of borrowings through four specified instruments, viz., term
deposits (TDs), term money borrowings, certificates of deposits (CDs), and
commercial papers (CPs). The terms and conditions relating to resource
mobilization by an AIFI through the various instruments shall be governed by the
norms prescribed in this Chapter.

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
logical_units.text:
B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B.1 Term Deposits (TDs)

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 7. An AIFI shall be permitted to raise TDs for a maturity period of not less than one
logical_units.text:
7. An AIFI shall be permitted to raise TDs for a maturity period of not less than one
year and not exceeding five years.
Provided that the TDs shall be of a minimum denomination of ₹10,000.
Provided further that the AIFI shall not pay brokerage exceeding one per cent of
the deposits accepted.

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 8. An AIFI shall not allow premature withdrawal of TDs before completion of one year.
logical_units.text:
8. An AIFI shall not allow premature withdrawal of TDs before completion of one year.
Provided that premature withdrawal of TDs before completion of one year shall be
permitted in case of death of the depositor, medical exigencies, educational
expenditure, and other such reasons, as approved by the Board of the AIFI.

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 9. An AIFI shall have the freedom to fix its own penal rate of interest on premature
logical_units.text:
9. An AIFI shall have the freedom to fix its own penal rate of interest on premature
withdrawal of deposits.
<image>
Provided that for deposits where premature withdrawal has been made before
completion of six months, no interest shall be payable to the depositor.
Provided further that for deposits where premature withdrawal has been made on
the date of or after completion of six months and before completion of one year,
interest shall be payable at the rate of average of the minimum and maximum
saving deposit rates as published in the Weekly Statistical Supplement (WSS) to
the monthly RBI Bulletin.

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 10. An AIFI shall mandatorily have its TDs rated from a SEBI-approved Credit Rating
logical_units.text:
10. An AIFI shall mandatorily have its TDs rated from a SEBI-approved Credit Rating
Agency.

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 11. An AIFI shall not be permitted to provide loans against the TDs accepted, unless
logical_units.text:
11. An AIFI shall not be permitted to provide loans against the TDs accepted, unless
specifically permitted by the RBI.
B.2 Term Money Borrowings

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 12. An AIFI shall be permitted to borrow through term money for a maturity period of
logical_units.text:
12. An AIFI shall be permitted to borrow through term money for a maturity period of
not less than three months and not exceeding six months.

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 13. An AIFI shall be free to fix the interest rates payable on the term money borrowings.
logical_units.text: 13. An AIFI shall be free to fix the interest rates payable on the term money borrowings.

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 14. An AIFI shall be permitted to borrow term money only from scheduled commercial
logical_units.text:
14. An AIFI shall be permitted to borrow term money only from scheduled commercial
banks and co-operative banks.
B.3 Certificates of Deposit (CDs)

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 15. CDs shall be issued only in dematerialised form and held with a depository
logical_units.text:
15. CDs shall be issued only in dematerialised form and held with a depository
registered with SEBI.

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 16. An AIFI shall be permitted to issue CDs to all persons resident in India.
logical_units.text: 16. An AIFI shall be permitted to issue CDs to all persons resident in India.

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 17. CDs shall be issued in a minimum denomination of ₹5 lakh and in multiples of ₹5
logical_units.text:
17. CDs shall be issued in a minimum denomination of ₹5 lakh and in multiples of ₹5
lakh thereafter.

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 18. The tenor of a CD at issuance shall not be less than one year and shall not exceed
logical_units.text:
18. The tenor of a CD at issuance shall not be less than one year and shall not exceed
three years.
Provided that the AIFI shall not be permitted to buy back its own CDs before
maturity.
Provided further that there shall be no grace period for repayment of CDs.
Provided further that no loans shall be sanctioned by an AIFI against its CDs,
unless specifically permitted by the Reserve Bank.
<image>

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 19. CDs shall be issued on a T+1 basis where T represents the date of closure of the
logical_units.text:
19. CDs shall be issued on a T+1 basis where T represents the date of closure of the
offer period for issuance of the CDs.

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 20. An AIFI shall be permitted to issue CDs at a discount to face value.
logical_units.text:
20. An AIFI shall be permitted to issue CDs at a discount to face value.
Provided that CDs shall be permitted to be issued on a fixed / floating rate basis.
Provided further that the interest rate on the floating rate CDs shall be reset at
periodic rests agreed to at the time of issue and shall be linked to a benchmark
published by a Financial Benchmark Administrator, as authorised by Reserve
Bank, or approved by the FIMMDA for this purpose.
Provided further that FIMMDA shall ensure that any floating rate approved by them
for this purpose is determined transparently, objectively, and in arm's length
transactions.

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 21. In the secondary market, CDs shall be traded either in Over the Counter (OTC)
logical_units.text:
21. In the secondary market, CDs shall be traded either in Over the Counter (OTC)
markets, including on Electronic Trading Platforms, or on recognised stock
exchanges with approval of the RBI.
Provided that the settlement cycle for OTC trades in CDs shall be T+0 or T+1.
Provided further that all secondary market transactions in CDs shall be settled on
a Delivery versus Payment (DvP) basis through the clearing corporation of any
recognized stock exchange or any other mechanism approved by the RBI.

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 22. Reporting requirements
logical_units.text:
22. Reporting requirements
(1) Details of primary issuance of a CD shall be reported by the issuer to the Trade
Repository (TR) of the Clearing Corporation of India Ltd. (CCIL) by 5:30 PM on the
day of issuance or as decided by the RBI.
(2) All secondary market transactions executed in the OTC market and / or on
recognised stock exchanges in CDs shall be reported, with time stamp, within 15
minutes of execution (the time when price is agreed) on the TR by each
counterparty to the transaction.
B.4 Commercial Papers (CPs)

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 23. An AIFI shall be governed by the instructions contained in Master Direction -
logical_units.text:
23. An AIFI shall be governed by the instructions contained in Master Direction -
Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Paper and Non-Convertible Debentures of
original or initial maturity upto one year) Directions, 2024.
<image>
B.5 Bonds

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 24. An AIFI shall be permitted to raise resources by way of issue of bonds, either by
logical_units.text:
24. An AIFI shall be permitted to raise resources by way of issue of bonds, either by
public issue or through private placement, without the prior approval of the RBI.
Provided that the minimum maturity of the bond at issuance shall be 3 years.
Provided further that the interest / coupon payable to the investors shall be
permitted to be either at a fixed rate or at a floating rate referenced to a market
determined benchmark rate.
Provided further that no call or put or exit options (by whatever name called) on the
bonds shall be exercisable before the expiry of one year from the date of issue of
the bonds

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 25. An AIFI shall ensure to comply with the provisions of any directions, regulations, or
logical_units.text:
25. An AIFI shall ensure to comply with the provisions of any directions, regulations, or
guidelines issued by any regulator or any other authority that may be applicable, in
respect of issue of all the above-mentioned instruments.
<image>

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word

logical_units.title: A. Repeal and Saving
logical_units.text: A. Repeal and Saving

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 26. With the issue of these Directions, the existing instructions / guidelines relating to
logical_units.text:
26. With the issue of these Directions, the existing instructions / guidelines relating to
Resource Raising Norms as applicable to All India Financial Institutions stand
repealed, as communicated vide circular DOR.RRC.REC.302/33-01-010/2025-26
dated November 28, 2025. The Directions, instructions and guidelines repealed
prior to the issuance of these Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word
toc: B. Specified Instruments for Resource Raising
B. Application of other laws not barred

logical_units.title: 27. Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken, or
logical_units.text:
27. Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken, or
initiated under the repealed instructions or guidelines shall continue to be governed
by the provisions thereof. All approvals or acknowledgments granted under these
repealed lists shall be deemed as governed by these Directions. Further, the repeal
of these directions, instructions, or guidelines shall not in any way prejudicially
affect:
(1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder;
(2) any, penalty, forfeiture, or punishment incurred in respect of any
contravention committed thereunder;
(3) any investigation, legal proceeding, or remedy in respect of any such right,
privilege, obligation, liability, penalty, forfeiture, or punishment as aforesaid;
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may
be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar
source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word

logical_units.title: 28. The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
logical_units.text:
28. The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or Directions, for the time being
in force.

source_doc_metadata.author: Rajesh Kumar

source_doc_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 25 for Word

logical_units.title: 29. For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
logical_units.text:
29. For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these
Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary clarifications in
respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any provision of
these Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.
<image>
(Sunil T S Nair)
Chief General Manager
